Tangled webs

March 2004
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsGovernment & Politics

IRE and NICAR are exploring social network analysis as a tool for journalists to visualize relationships between individuals, businesses, and institutions, a technique long used by law enforcement and other professionals. The Washington Post demonstrated its effectiveness in 2003 when investigating visa mill language schools.

IRE and NICAR are beginning to more deeply explore social network analysis for visualizing and diagramming relationships between individuals, busi- nesses and institutions. It's a new approach for journalists, but business consultants, law enforcement agencies and pub- lic health investigators have used this approach for decades. In 2003, The Washington Post used social network analysis to report on a group of Los Ange- les-area language schools that appeared to be "visa mills" for for- eign students.

For more informa- tion and a list of Web links and tipsheets on this emerging tech- nique, go to www.ire.org/sna. INSIDE NICAR
